title: Load and Explore a Dataset
description: Load the Iris dataset into a pandas DataFrame, explore its structure using head(), info(), and describe().
starter_code:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

# Load iris into DataFrame
iris = load_iris()
# TODO: Create DataFrame with iris data and column names
df = None  # Replace with pd.DataFrame(...)

print(df.head())
print(df.info())
print(df.describe())
solution:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)
df['target'] = iris.target

print(df.head())
print(df.info())
print(df.describe())
hints: Add target as a new column

title: Filter and Select Data
description: Filter rows where sepal length > 5.0 and display only sepal length and sepal width columns.
starter_code:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)

# TODO: Filter rows where sepal length > 5.0
# TODO: Select only first two columns
result = None  # Replace with filtered DataFrame
solution:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)

# Filter and select
result = df[df['sepal length (cm)'] > 5.0][['sepal length (cm)', 'sepal width (cm)']]
print(result.head())
print(f"Shape: {result.shape}")
hints: Select columns with double brackets: df[['col1', 'col2']]

title: Handle Missing Values
description: Create a DataFrame with missing values, then demonstrate filling and dropping missing values.
starter_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.DataFrame({
    'A': [1, 2, np.nan, 4],
    'B': [5, np.nan, 7, 8],
    'C': [9, 10, 11, 12]
})

print("Missing values:")
print(df.isnull().sum())

# TODO: Drop rows with any missing
df_dropped = None

# TODO: Fill missing with column mean
df_filled = None

print(df_dropped)
print(df_filled)
solution:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.DataFrame({
    'A': [1, 2, np.nan, 4],
    'B': [5, np.nan, 7, 8],
    'C': [9, 10, 11, 12]
})

print("Missing values:")
print(df.isnull().sum())

# Drop rows with any missing
df_dropped = df.dropna()

# Fill missing with column mean
df_filled = df.fillna(df.mean())

print(df_dropped)
print(df_filled)

title: Group By and Aggregate
description: Load Iris data and compute mean sepal length for each flower species (target).
starter_code:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)
df['species'] = iris.target_names[iris.target]

# TODO: Group by species and compute mean sepal length
result = None  # Replace with groupby result

print(result)
solution:
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)
df['species'] = iris.target_names[iris.target]

# Group by species and compute mean sepal length
result = df.groupby('species')['sepal length (cm)'].mean()

print(result)

starter_code:
import numpy as np

# Create array
arr = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])

# TODO: Add 10 to each element
arr_plus_10 = None

# TODO: Square each element
arr_squared = None

# TODO: Compute mean and std
mean_val = None

print(f"Plus 10: {arr_plus_10}")

solution:
import numpy as np

arr = np.array([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])

# Add 10 to each element
arr_plus_10 = arr + 10

# Square each element
arr_squared = arr ** 2

# Compute mean and std
mean_val = arr.mean()
std_val = arr.std()

print(f"Plus 10: {arr_plus_10}")
print(f"Squared: {arr_squared}")
print(f"Mean: {mean_val}, Std: {std_val}")

title: Create Basic Plots
description: Use matplotlib to create line plot and scatter plot of Iris sepal data.
starter_code:

import pandas as pd
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)

# TODO: Create figure with 2 subplots
fig, axes = None  # Replace with plt.subplots(1, 2)

# TODO: Line plot on first subplot
# axes[0].plot(...)

# TODO: Scatter plot on second subplot
# axes[1].scatter(...)

plt.show()
solution:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)

# Create figure with 2 subplots
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 4))

# Line plot on first subplot
axes[0].plot(df['sepal length (cm)'], linewidth=2)
axes[0].set_title('Sepal Length (Line)')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Length')

# Scatter plot on second subplot
axes[1].scatter(df['sepal length (cm)'], df['sepal width (cm)'], alpha=0.5)
axes[1].set_title('Sepal Length vs Width')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Length')
axes[1].set_ylabel('Width')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
hints: Use plt.subplots(rows, cols) for multiple plots

title: Heatmap for Correlation
description: Create a correlation matrix heatmap using seaborn.
starter_code:
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)

# TODO: Compute correlation matrix
corr = None  # Replace with df.corr()

# TODO: Create heatmap
# sns.heatmap(...)

plt.show()
solution:
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)

# Compute correlation matrix
corr = df.corr()

# Create heatmap
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
sns.heatmap(corr, annot=True, cmap='coolwarm', square=True)
plt.title('Feature Correlation Matrix')
plt.show()

title: Detect Outliers with IQR
description: Detect outliers using the Interquartile Range (IQR) method.
starter_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.DataFrame({
    'value': [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 100, 6, 7, 8, 9]
})

# TODO: Calculate Q1, Q3, and IQR
Q1 = None
Q3 = None
IQR = None

# TODO: Find outliers (values < Q1 - 1.5*IQR or > Q3 + 1.5*IQR)
outliers = None

print(f"Q1: {Q1}, Q3: {Q3}, IQR: {IQR}")
print(f"Outliers:\n{outliers}")
solution:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.DataFrame({
    'value': [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 100, 6, 7, 8, 9]
})

# Calculate Q1, Q3, and IQR
Q1 = df['value'].quantile(0.25)
Q3 = df['value'].quantile(0.75)
IQR = Q3 - Q1

# Find outliers
outliers = df[(df['value'] < Q1 - 1.5*IQR) | (df['value'] > Q3 + 1.5*IQR)]

print(f"Q1: {Q1}, Q3: {Q3}, IQR: {IQR}")
print(f"Outliers:\n{outliers}")

title: Complete EDA Workflow
description: Perform full EDA: load, explore, check missing, detect outliers, and create visualization.
starter_code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)
df['species'] = iris.target_names[iris.target]

# Step 1: Explore
print(f"Shape: {df.shape}")
print(df.head())

# Step 2: Check missing
print(f"Missing: {df.isnull().sum().sum()}")

# Step 3: Describe
print(df.describe())

# Step 4: Correlation
corr = None  # TODO: Get correlation

# Step 5: Visualize distributions
fig, axes = None  # TODO: Create 2x2 subplots
solution:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

iris = load_iris()
df = pd.DataFrame(iris.data, columns=iris.feature_names)
df['species'] = iris.target_names[iris.target]

# Step 1: Explore
print(f"Shape: {df.shape}")
print(df.head())

# Step 2: Check missing
print(f"Missing: {df.isnull().sum().sum()}")

# Step 3: Describe
print(df.describe())

# Step 4: Correlation
corr = df.iloc[:, :-1].corr()
print(corr)

# Step 5: Visualize distributions
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(10, 8))
numeric_cols = df.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns
for idx, col in enumerate(numeric_cols[:4]):
    ax = axes[idx // 2, idx % 2]
    ax.hist(df[col], bins=20, edgecolor='black')
    ax.set_title(f'Distribution of {col}')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
